explanation_md: `b.area()` is the inherited `Shape::area()` which returns `4.0 * 3.0 = 12`. `b.volume()` is the new `Box` function that calls `area()` (which returns 12) and multiplies by `depth` (2.0), giving `24`.

prompt_md: Can a new member function added to a derived class call inherited member functions from the base class?

options.a: No — derived-only functions can only access derived-only members
options.b: Yes — derived functions have access to all public and protected members, including inherited ones
options.c: Only if the derived function is also declared in the base class
options.d: Only if you use the base class name as a qualifier: `Base::function()`

explanation_md: A derived class member function can freely call any public or protected inherited member function (and access any public or protected inherited data member). No special syntax is needed — the inherited function is in scope just like a member of the derived class.

options.a: `describe()` tries to access `type` which is only available in `Car`, not `Vehicle`
options.b: `describe()` is defined in `Car`, not `Vehicle`. Calling it on a `Vehicle` object is an error
options.c: `std::string` members cannot be used in a class without including `<string>`
options.d: `int doors = 4` requires a constructor to be valid

explanation_md: `describe()` is a member function of `Car`, not `Vehicle`. A `Vehicle` object has no `describe()` method. The base class does not inherit functions from its derived classes — the relationship only flows downward. To call `describe()`, `v` must be declared as `Car`.

prompt_md: A derived class `Truck` inherits from `Vehicle`. `Vehicle` has `void start()`. `Truck` adds `void loadCargo()`. Which calls are valid on a `Truck` object `t`?

options.a: Only `t.loadCargo()` — derived objects can only use derived-class functions
options.d: Neither — you must cast `t` to access any function

explanation_md: A derived object has access to all public inherited functions from the base class AND all public functions defined in the derived class. `t.start()` calls the inherited `Vehicle::start()`, and `t.loadCargo()` calls the `Truck`-specific function.
